short_name.description:
Short name of the problem. Usually, just an uppercase letter,
but can be a sequence of uppercase letters followed by a number.
short_name.title: Short Name

aliases.description:
Optional list of aliases for this problem. You can refer to the problem by its
short_name or by any of these aliases in commands such as [item]rbx on <name> run[/item].
Aliases must be unique across all problems (case-insensitive).
aliases.title: Aliases

path.description:
Path to the problem relative to the contest package directory.
If not specified, will expect the problem to be in ./{short_name}/ folder.
path.title: Path

color.description:
Color that represents this problem in the contest.

Can be a hex color (#abcdef or #abc format), or a color name among available X11 colors.

See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X11_color_names for the list of supported color names.
color.title: Color

colorName.description:
A custom color name for the color provided by this problem.

If not provided, will try to infer a color name from the color provided.
colorName.title: Colorname

ContestStatement.description:
A contest-level statement. Owns the templates used to render problems both
standalone and inside the contest join (design §3.2).

variant.description: Optional discriminator between formats of the same language. Together with `language` it forms the join key with contest statements.
variant.title: Variant

title.description: Title as it appears in the statement. Can be left unset to fall back to the package/contest title.
title.title: Title

file.description: Path to the input statement file. Required unless this statement `extends` another one to inherit its file.
file.title: File

type.description: Type of the input statement file.

params.description: This statement's own parameters, exposed to the template as the `params` namespace (kept separate from problem/contest `vars`).

samples.description: Whether to build the statement with samples.
samples.title: Samples

assets.description: Globs (relative to the package root) selecting files to ship as statement resources (e.g. images/PDFs). Inherited via `extends`. At build time the default image/PDF globs over the statement subtree and each sample subtree are concatenated to this list.
assets.title: Assets

name.description: Name of this statement. Unique within the contest.
name.title: Name

extends.description: Name of the contest statement to inherit the build recipe from.
extends.title: Extends

location.description: Location of the contest in this language.
location.title: Location

date.description: Date of the contest in this language.
date.title: Date

standaloneProblemTemplate.description: Template applied to build a problem-level statement as a standalone document (`rbx st b`). rbx* types only.
standaloneProblemTemplate.title: Standaloneproblemtemplate

contestProblemTemplate.description: Template applied to build the problem fragment that gets imported into the contest statement (`rbx contest st b`). rbx* types only.
contestProblemTemplate.title: Contestproblemtemplate

ContestStatement.title: ContestStatement

Document.description:
A contest-level document (infosheet, etc.). Shares the statement model but
NEVER joins on problems, so it is restricted to non-rbx types (design §3.2).

variant.description: Optional discriminator between formats of the same language. Together with `language` it forms the join key with contest statements.
variant.title: Variant

title.description: Title as it appears in the statement. Can be left unset to fall back to the package/contest title.
title.title: Title

file.description: Path to the input statement file. Required unless this statement `extends` another one to inherit its file.
file.title: File

type.description: Type of the input statement file.

params.description: This statement's own parameters, exposed to the template as the `params` namespace (kept separate from problem/contest `vars`).

samples.description: Whether to build the statement with samples.
samples.title: Samples

assets.description: Globs (relative to the package root) selecting files to ship as statement resources (e.g. images/PDFs). Inherited via `extends`. At build time the default image/PDF globs over the statement subtree and each sample subtree are concatenated to this list.
assets.title: Assets

name.description: Name of this document. Unique within the contest.
name.title: Name

extends.description: Name of the document to inherit the build recipe from.
extends.title: Extends

Document.title: Document

use_variants.description: When true, this file is a sentinel marking the directory as a multi-contest dispatcher. The actual contests live in sibling files matching contest.<id>.rbx.yml. When set, no other Contest fields may be specified.
use_variants.title: Use Variants

titles.description: Titles for the contest in each language. Languages should be specified as lowercase ISO 639-1 codes.
titles.title: Titles

problems.description: List of problems in this contest.
problems.title: Problems

statements.description: Configure statements in this contest, per language.
statements.title: Statements

tutorials.description: Configure tutorials (editorials) in this contest, per language.
tutorials.title: Tutorials

documents.description: Configure standalone documents (infosheets, etc.) for this contest. Documents never join on problems.
documents.title: Documents

vars.description: Variables to be re-used across the package.
